evals.prompt: /boardroom Should we hire 2 senior engineers ($180K each) or 4 junior engineers ($95K each) for our next quarter?
expectations: Creates a decision folder with timestamp
Generates analysis.md with vote tracker showing Round 1 and Round 2 votes
Each advisor provides 800-1200 words in Round 1
Each advisor provides 400-800 words in Round 2
Round 2 responses reference specific arguments from other advisors
At least one advisor changes their vote or confidence between rounds
Includes specific cost projections for both scenarios
Includes team impact analysis (velocity, morale, mentorship needs)
Identifies at least 3 key trade-offs
Generates interactive HTML dashboard with working sliders
HTML includes all 4 advisor cards with personality profiles
Generates PDF summary
Final synthesis identifies biggest fight and sharpest insight
Provides clear recommended decision

evals.prompt: /boardroom Should we double our prices to position as premium or keep current pricing to maximize growth?
expectations: Analyzes revenue impact under both scenarios
Considers customer retention vs acquisition trade-off
Includes competitor pricing analysis
Projects different scenarios (best case, base case, worst case)
At least one advisor takes contrarian position
Addresses brand positioning implications
Considers enterprise vs self-serve segment impact differently
Round 2 rebuttals include updated financial projections
Dashboard includes price point slider with dynamic revenue calculations
Identifies specific pricing experiments to test assumptions

evals.prompt: /boardroom Should we raise Series A now at $500K ARR or wait 6 months to hit $1M ARR?
expectations: Analyzes runway implications of waiting
Considers competitive fundraising landscape
Includes valuation implications for both scenarios
Addresses dilution trade-offs
At least one advisor discusses investor psychology and market timing
Considers impact on team morale and hiring ability
Projects burn rate and cash position for both scenarios
Identifies specific milestones that matter most to investors
Round 2 includes debate about revenue as proof point
Final recommendation includes specific conditions (e.g., if burn rate hits X, raise early)

evals.prompt: /boardroom Should we build our own vector database from scratch or continue using pgvector?
expectations: Analyzes engineering effort required (person-months)
Considers opportunity cost vs feature development
Includes technical complexity and risk assessment
Projects performance and scalability implications
Addresses competitive moat considerations
At least one advisor emphasizes time-to-market risk
Includes infrastructure cost implications
Round 2 includes debate about first-principles vs pragmatism
Dashboard includes complexity slider affecting timeline estimates
Identifies specific performance benchmarks that would trigger the decision
Recognizes question is too vague
Asks clarifying questions about what type of expansion (team, market, product)
Provides 3-5 specific expansion options to choose from
Does not proceed with full boardroom analysis until question is clarified
Suggests using business context to propose likely expansions
